title: Married To The Blob

jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.punchline: Homer's signature frustrated exclamation
jokes.explanation: Iconic character moment with perfect economy, though routine by this point in the series

jokes.text: French Femme Fatale Charcoal Briquette and the enemy of drinking water everywhere, the Fracker
jokes.punchline: Mixing classic comic villain names with modern environmental issues
jokes.explanation: Clever environmental satire through comic book pastiche, good wordplay

jokes.text: Those studies were industry financed. You lie!
jokes.setup: Villain claims studies prove nuclear is safest
jokes.punchline: Hero immediately calls out industry bias
jokes.explanation: Sharp political satire with good comic timing, relevant environmental commentary

jokes.text: It's too cloudy. People don't like the noise I make. Ehhh...
jokes.setup: Superhero calling for renewable energy backup
jokes.punchline: Green energy heroes admit their practical limitations
jokes.explanation: Brilliant environmental satire - perfectly captures real renewable energy challenges through superhero lens

jokes.text: I have become my destiny
jokes.setup: Being reminded of his former identity as environmental scientist
jokes.punchline: Melodramatic acceptance of his villainous transformation
jokes.explanation: Good villainous pomposity, plays on environmental scientist turned corporate shill trope

jokes.text: Half life. Quar...ter life. Eighth life. Gone. Frack-cellent!
jokes.punchline: Nuclear decay terms + fracking pun celebration
jokes.explanation: Clever scientific wordplay with good escalating structure, memorable pun

jokes.text: Die-carumba!
jokes.punchline: Death pun on Bart's catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Solid character-specific pun, perfect economy though not highly original

jokes.text: They'll just reboot Radioactive Man in an overpriced new origin issue and get clueless fanboys like you to line up at midnight to buy them. Clueless fanboys in costume.
jokes.setup: Bart shocked that they killed off Radioactive Man
jokes.punchline: CBG's cynical take on comic industry reboots and fan exploitation
jokes.explanation: Perfect CBG character moment - cynical comic industry insider knowledge with condescending delivery

jokes.text: I won't know which day to write in my diary
jokes.punchline: Bart's innocent confusion about midnight timing
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment showing Bart's childish perspective on time

jokes.text: That's when he experiments, boy
jokes.setup: Bart asking to miss back half of Jimmy Kimmel
jokes.punchline: Homer treating late-night TV like serious artistic endeavor
jokes.explanation: Great Homer logic - treating throwaway late-night content as high art with perfect confidence

jokes.text: After the Oscars, when he forces you
jokes.setup: Homer claiming he watches Jimmy Kimmel
jokes.punchline: Bart pointing out Homer only watches when forced by Oscar coverage
jokes.explanation: Sharp comeback exposing Homer's TV viewing habits, good family dynamics

jokes.text: Tell it to Harlan Ellison
jokes.setup: Someone wanting to go first in line because they were cast as Fallout Boy
jokes.punchline: Dismissive reference to famously difficult sci-fi writer
jokes.explanation: Perfect CBG reference - obscure enough to be authentic, dismissive enough to be characteristic

jokes.text: I think, you over-privileged kids with your electronic tablets and your talking phones, wouldn't know a good piece of fiction if your iPod read it to your ass
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific insult ending with anatomical reference
jokes.explanation: Classic curmudgeonly CBG rant with escalating absurdity, perfect character voice

jokes.text: I wish someone would have come from the future, and warned me not to talk to you. That's my idea! You're stealing my idea!
jokes.punchline: Customer immediately claiming ownership of the concept
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing fan obsessiveness about intellectual property, realistic comic shop dynamics

jokes.text: Or should I say, 'Foolsville'
jokes.punchline: Childish name-calling wordplay
jokes.explanation: Weak pun but perfectly captures CBG's petty competitiveness and juvenile spite

jokes.text: I wrote a Lost fan fiction finale that also answers all the questions from Twin Peaks. Reranked all the DoctorWhos by puffiness of the hair
jokes.punchline: Increasingly specific and absurd nerd tasks
jokes.explanation: Perfect nerd character specificity, excellent escalation from ambitious to trivial pursuits

jokes.text: non-imaginary, comic-tolerating girlfriend, Strawberry
jokes.punchline: Self-aware qualifications that reveal his insecurity
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing nerd awareness of their social situation, good specificity

jokes.text: Now when Milo talks to himself, it won't seem crazy
jokes.punchline: CBG's bitter observation about social acceptability of self-talk
jokes.explanation: Sharp insight into social dynamics of loneliness, good character bitterness

jokes.text: Even the mintiest comic is just acidifying pulp, if you have no one to read it with, right?
jokes.setup: Milo explaining why he's sharing comics with his bride
jokes.punchline: Unexpectedly poetic metaphor about loneliness using comic book terminology
jokes.explanation: Beautiful metaphor mixing comic collecting with human connection, surprisingly touching

jokes.text: Comic-Con is now eight percent women. Burn!
jokes.setup: CBG saying nerds don't get girls
jokes.punchline: Celebrating pathetically small percentage as victory
jokes.explanation: Perfect example of delusional optimism, excellent economy with 'Burn!' celebration

jokes.text: We're like George Burns and John Denver
jokes.setup: Homer describing his relationship with Marge
jokes.punchline: Completely nonsensical comparison to unrelated celebrities
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer non-sequitur, perfect confidence in meaningless comparison

jokes.text: Jimmy Olsen vs. Matter Eater Lad. Hm. This is better than I remembered, actually. No...! No it isn't! It-It's horrible!
jokes.setup: CBG finding old comic to distract himself
jokes.punchline: Rapid emotional reversal as loneliness overrides objective judgment
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing depression affecting critical judgment, good emotional escalation

jokes.text: The only thing that could make this moment more cliched, is if I started to sing about my feelings. And... here I go.
jokes.punchline: Immediately following through on the exact cliché he just identified
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-commentary with perfect execution - acknowledging the trope while embracing it

jokes.text: You could say that I was an unstackable Pringle
jokes.punchline: Chip metaphor for social incompatibility
jokes.explanation: Brilliant metaphor with perfect economy - captures both physical and social aspects of his situation

jokes.text: With your cardboard stand of Scarlett Johansson!
jokes.setup: CBG singing about his lonely nights
jokes.punchline: Revealing pathetic romantic substitute
jokes.explanation: Effective cringe humor revealing character's sad reality, good specificity with celebrity choice

jokes.text: Dropped my heart into Mylar and then... Vacuum sealed... it
jokes.punchline: Comic book preservation metaphor for emotional withdrawal
jokes.explanation: Absolutely brilliant character-specific metaphor perfectly integrating hobby with emotional state

jokes.text: I'm your imagination. The real Stan Lee's over there. Excelsior!
jokes.punchline: Imaginary Stan Lee pointing out the real one, who delivers his catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Smart meta-joke about cameo appearances, good setup for Stan Lee's actual involvement

jokes.text: But now... your opportunity is knocking
jokes.setup: Stan Lee talking to CBG about his romantic prospects
jokes.punchline: Kumiko literally knocking on the door at that moment
jokes.explanation: Classic sitcom timing coincidence, well-executed but predictable

jokes.text: Hey, I'm 90 years old. I can do what I want
jokes.setup: Being told he's interfering when he said he could only watch
jokes.punchline: Age-based justification for rule-breaking
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stan Lee voice - playful rule-breaking with age as excuse, good economy

jokes.text: The Just Us League? You would be member number 003. I-- I am the first two
jokes.setup: CBG offering membership in frequent buyer's club
jokes.punchline: Revealing he's the only member with multiple identities
jokes.explanation: Great character moment revealing loneliness through business desperation, good Justice League pun

jokes.text: I am visiting America's saddest cities, as research for my autobiographical manga. Volume Six, Springfield. Land of Angry Tears
jokes.setup: Kumiko explaining her presence in Springfield
jokes.punchline: Springfield classified as sad city for manga research
jokes.explanation: Sharp commentary on Springfield's reputation, good character introduction with specific purpose

jokes.text: You're either looking down in disgust, or up in disdain
jokes.setup: CBG asking if she's seen him before
jokes.punchline: Describing his characteristic expressions of superiority
jokes.explanation: Perfect character observation with great structure - captures CBG's snobby essence precisely

jokes.text: She tolerates me!
jokes.punchline: CBG is thrilled by basic tolerance
jokes.explanation: Perfect CBG low expectations and gratitude for minimal acceptance

jokes.text: Is it too soon to give you flowers in a Hulk hand?
jokes.punchline: Nerdy twist on traditional romantic gesture
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing how he'd adapt romance to his world, charming awkwardness

jokes.text: The closest thing I have to a father is the Obi-Wan doll in my store. And he comes from a race of celibate knights
jokes.setup: CBG asking Homer for dating help
jokes.punchline: Explaining his lack of male role models through Star Wars reference
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment revealing pathetic backstory through perfect pop culture lens

jokes.text: Because you are the only fat man in real life who ever got a hot woman
jokes.setup: Homer asking why CBG chose him for help
jokes.punchline: Brutally honest assessment of Homer's romantic success
jokes.explanation: Sharp character insight with good economy, captures CBG's blunt analytical nature

jokes.text: It helps if she's got a mother she's trying to get back at
jokes.punchline: Cynical take on Marge's motivations
jokes.explanation: Dark but insightful relationship observation with perfect Homer logic

jokes.text: Don't be the way you normally are and sustain that for the rest of your life
jokes.punchline: Advice escalating to impossible long-term commitment
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing the unsustainable nature of dating pretense

jokes.text: In my country, it is a tradition to bring a much older couple on a first date. It is not in any way, weird, or-or sad
jokes.punchline: Kumiko's obvious lie about cultural tradition
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Kumiko's kindness in going along with awkward situation

jokes.text: Does anyone tell you that you look like the man on a box of Mr. Sparkle?
jokes.setup: Marge making conversation with Kumiko's father
jokes.punchline: Reference to classic Simpsons Mr. Sparkle episode
jokes.explanation: Strong callback to beloved classic episode, high impact for fans

jokes.text: In Japan, drinking your detergent is a popular method of suicide
jokes.punchline: Extremely dark non-sequitur delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Shockingly dark humor delivered with perfect deadpan, creates awkward silence

jokes.text: But the walls are covered with garbage. Oh... Not garbage, Americana
jokes.punchline: Homer's correction distinguishing junk from cultural artifacts
jokes.explanation: Good satire of American restaurant kitsch culture, nice correction structure

jokes.text: They're neither in alphabetical, nor geographical order. What-What madness is this?
jokes.punchline: CBG's obsessive need for systematic organization
jokes.explanation: Perfect CBG character moment showing his need for order in chaos

jokes.text: In Japan, no one ever says what they think. We know our game shows are degrading and our baseball fences are too close. But no one says anything
jokes.punchline: Specific examples of Japanese cultural politeness preventing criticism
jokes.explanation: Good cultural observation with specific examples, shows character insight

jokes.text: You should've spent less on the kitsch, and more on the kitch-en
jokes.punchline: Kitchen/kitsch wordplay while insulting the food
jokes.explanation: Solid wordplay with restaurant criticism, good economy

jokes.text: American nerd snark is the finest in the world. Let's go walking and mocking in the rain
jokes.punchline: Romantic parody of 'Singing in the Rain' with mockery
jokes.explanation: Brilliant romantic moment combining cultural observation with perfect movie reference parody

jokes.text: Father, why-sa you-sa forsake-a me-sa?
jokes.setup: Biblical reference in stereotypical Italian accent
jokes.punchline: Adding 'sa' suffix to every word
jokes.explanation: Weak stereotypical humor relying on accent comedy, lazy execution

jokes.text: You have nurtured our love like Steven Speilberg nurtured the careers of Zemeckis and Gale
jokes.punchline: Overly specific Hollywood producer comparison for simple help
jokes.explanation: Perfect CBG specificity applying film industry knowledge to personal situation

jokes.text: At last, we'll be in the same bed. Just like Batman and Robin
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate superhero comparison for romantic relationship
jokes.explanation: Effective cringe humor showing CBG's social awkwardness through comic book lens

jokes.text: Apparently, including tying the knots on the hammock
jokes.setup: Homer claiming hammock is chore-free zone
jokes.punchline: Hammock falling apart from lack of maintenance
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy setup showing consequences of Homer's laziness

jokes.text: And getting the termites out of the trees
jokes.punchline: Escalating to environmental tree maintenance
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing extent of Homer's neglect affecting the environment

jokes.text: I am not a Kung fu instructor. I am a humble salary man who could kick your ass
jokes.punchline: Humble self-description followed by threat
jokes.explanation: Great character moment with perfect contradiction between humility and aggression

jokes.text: I was going to say, like you're going to have a stroke. I get that a lot too
jokes.punchline: Homer's medical observation and father's casual acceptance
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with dark humor, casual acceptance of health concerns

jokes.text: So, an obese nerd has stolen my daughter to live in his basement? I didn't tell you about the basement. It was an obvious guess!
jokes.punchline: Revealing his accurate stereotype-based deduction
jokes.explanation: Sharp observation about nerd stereotypes being predictably accurate

jokes.text: I didn't tell you about the basement. It was an obvious guess!
jokes.punchline: CBG admits his life is stereotypically predictable
jokes.explanation: Good self-awareness of stereotypical nerd living situation

jokes.text: You can retrieve rants if you did not empty your trash
jokes.setup: CBG saying he deleted rants from his blog
jokes.punchline: Technical computer advice during emotional moment
jokes.explanation: Perfect CBG moment - unable to stop being technical even during heartbreak

jokes.text: Now I know I've got a heart... because it's breaking. Wizard of Oz reference! Did I do that? Urkle reference! Help me. Fire reference! I can't stop
jokes.punchline: Compulsively identifying his own pop culture references
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character breakdown - showing how his pop culture obsession has become involuntary tic

jokes.text: No... discipline would imply, I'm trying to make him better
jokes.punchline: Homer's honest admission about his parenting approach
jokes.explanation: Dark but honest parenting observation with perfect Homer logic and economy

jokes.text: You promise double suicide. You hear what you want to hear. Always with me
jokes.punchline: Clarifying misunderstanding about suicide pact vs. spiritual presence
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with cultural misunderstanding, good misdirection structure

jokes.text: Now, that's disgusting. Wine made of rice?
jokes.punchline: Focusing on rice ingredient instead of snake
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - missing the obvious problem and focusing on the mundane

jokes.text: That was the aquarium
jokes.punchline: Revealing he drank from an aquarium
jokes.explanation: Great escalation with perfect economy - shows Homer's complete lack of judgment

jokes.text: You're the tail! No... You're the tail
jokes.punchline: Arguing over who's the less important body part
jokes.explanation: Basic physical comedy argument, functional but not exceptional

jokes.text: Dad! Bart's feet are on my half of the shell!
jokes.punchline: Maintaining sibling dynamics despite physical transformation
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency - sibling conflicts persist despite magical transformation

jokes.text: Hey, that actually feels good. Oh, yeah. More. A little bit more. Right there. Oh, yeah. Oh, yeah. That's the stuff.
jokes.setup: Characters are fighting as connected creatures
jokes.punchline: Homer enjoys the physical contact inappropriately
jokes.explanation: Relies on innuendo for humor, feels cheap and forced

jokes.text: The monster is me. Who... 'da thunk it?
jokes.punchline: Self-aware recognition with casual delivery
jokes.explanation: Good meta moment with character growth, casual delivery of self-awareness

jokes.text: They're giant polluters. But... the CEO is a woman. It's very vexing
jokes.setup: CBG describing his new job at phosphate works
jokes.punchline: Environmental concern vs. progressive values conflict
jokes.explanation: Smart character moment showing internal conflict between different progressive values

jokes.text: But then my seller rating will go down...
jokes.setup: Being told to cancel eBay sale
jokes.punchline: Prioritizing online reputation over romantic gesture
jokes.explanation: Perfect modern nerd priorities - online reputation more important than love

jokes.text: 'Best... Day... Ever.'
jokes.punchline: Using his signature critical phrase for genuine positivity
jokes.explanation: Beautiful character growth moment - transforming his sarcastic catchphrase into genuine expression of love

jokes.text: Ghost Wife, are you happy? Kind of!
jokes.punchline: Underwhelming ghostly response
jokes.explanation: Good supernatural comedy with perfect underwhelming response, great economy

jokes.text: Give us your eggs. I'm a boy. We said eggs
jokes.punchline: Insisting despite biological impossibility
jokes.explanation: Absurd logic comedy with good economy, creatures ignoring basic biology

tmdb_overview: When Comic Book Guy meets a woman writing an autobiographical manga, he seeks Homer's advice on dating.
